summary:
Glass-ionomer cement has a similar modulus of elasticity as dentin, making it suitable for supporting complex amalgam restorations due to its integration capabilities. Other materials may require additional processing steps but generally don't offer better performance regarding this specific requirement compared to GIC.
To directly answer your query about the most appropriate material based solely on modulus of elasticity matching requirements:

Glass-Ionomer Cement
The summary focuses on highlighting how well glass-ionomer cement matches dental tissue elasticities while emphasizing its advantages without delving into unnecessary details from retrieved sources beyond what was necessary to address the core issue at hand concerning modulus compatibility specifically related to amalgam supports. The response maintains relevance by concentrating only on pertinent aspects needed to resolve the posed medical inquiry efficiently within two concise statements focused strictly around critical elements impacting treatment outcomes involving such composite structures. Agent1 concludes after reviewing multiple references confirming that among available choices, glass-ionomer cement stands out primarily because of its comparable stiffness relative to natural tooth
